titleStmt.title: The Chairman of the American Delegation to the General Disarmament Conference ( (Davis) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Chairman of the American Delegation to the General Disarmament Conference ( Davis ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 876. BarthouJean Louis Barthou, French Minister for Foreign Affairs. tells me that he and LitvinoffMaxim Litvinov, Soviet Commissar for Foreign Affairs. have reached preliminary agreement and reduced to writing certain aspects of an “Eastern Locarno”, that Barthou has discussed this with BeckJosef Beck, Polish Minister for Foreign Affairs. who was non-committal in his attitude and will seek instructions, that Litvinoff is shortly going through Berlin and proposed to apprise the Germans of what was going on. Barthou requested permission to do it himself and is this afternoon cabling details to François-PoncetAndré François-Poncet, French Ambassador in Germany. together with the assurance that the earnest desire of both Governments is to have Germany adhere to this Eastern Locarno.

From French sources it is reported that the larger plan is that France will recognize German rearmament as a further inducement to Germany to enter the Eastern Locarno and then return to the ConferenceGeneral Disarmament Conference; for correspondence, see pp. 1 ff. and the League of Nations on an equal footing and with equality of rights thus recognized.
